verses.text: Moses to the heads of the tribes — this is the thing the LORD commanded. If a man vow a vow unto the LORD, or swear an oath, he shall not break his word; he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth. Guidance: A man's word is bond. No release, no rethinking — what came out of his mouth must walk out of his hands. Vows to God are not opening bids; they are sealed deeds. The chapter begins by binding the man tightest of all.

Moses to the heads of the tribes — this is the thing the LORD commanded. If a man vow a vow unto the LORD, or swear an oath, he shall not break his word; he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth. Guidance: A man's word is bond. No release, no rethinking — what came out of his mouth must walk out of his hands. Vows to God are not opening bids; they are sealed deeds. The chapter begins by binding the man tightest of all.

If a young woman in her father's house vow unto the LORD, and her father hear and hold his peace — every vow shall stand. If her father disallow her in the day he hears, none of her vows shall stand; the LORD shall forgive her. Guidance: A protective window, not a permanent veto. The father must speak the day he hears — not weeks later. Silence equals consent; her vow stands. If he overrules, God Himself absolves her — she is not held to a vow her family disallowed. The youngest voice gets a covering, not a cage.

If a young woman in her father's house vow unto the LORD, and her father hear and hold his peace — every vow shall stand. If her father disallow her in the day he hears, none of her vows shall stand; the LORD shall forgive her. Guidance: A protective window, not a permanent veto. The father must speak the day he hears — not weeks later. Silence equals consent; her vow stands. If he overrules, God Himself absolves her — she is not held to a vow her family disallowed. The youngest voice gets a covering, not a cage.

If a young woman in her father's house vow unto the LORD, and her father hear and hold his peace — every vow shall stand. If her father disallow her in the day he hears, none of her vows shall stand; the LORD shall forgive her. Guidance: A protective window, not a permanent veto. The father must speak the day he hears — not weeks later. Silence equals consent; her vow stands. If he overrules, God Himself absolves her — she is not held to a vow her family disallowed. The youngest voice gets a covering, not a cage.

If she had a husband when she vowed, and her husband heard and held his peace in the day he heard — her vows stand. If her husband disallowed her in the day he heard, he made void the vow she uttered — and the LORD shall forgive her. Guidance: Marriage doesn't erase her vows — but it gives the husband a same-day veto, parallel to the father's. Two rules anchor it: the timer is one day, and the LORD personally releases her. God's law treats a wife's oath as real enough to weigh.

If her husband disallowed her in the day he heard, he made void the vow she uttered — and the LORD shall forgive her. Guidance: Marriage doesn't erase her vows — but it gives the husband a same-day veto, parallel to the father's. Two rules anchor it: the timer is one day, and the LORD personally releases her. God's law treats a wife's oath as real enough to weigh.

Every vow of a widow and of her that is divorced, wherewith they have bound their souls, shall stand against her. Guidance: One verse — and a quiet recognition. The widow and the divorced woman answer to no one but God. Her word, her vow, her standing. No human veto, no waiting on a man to hear. Society may not have known what to do with her; the law of God did.

If she vowed in her husband's house and her husband held peace — all her vows shall stand. If he make them void after he heard — he shall bear her iniquity. Every vow, every binding oath to afflict the soul, her husband may establish or void. If he hold his peace day by day, he establisheth them. Guidance: The husband cannot wait a week and then cancel her vow — if he hears and stays silent, he has confirmed it. If he voids it late, the guilt is HIS, not hers. The law fences in the husband's authority, not the wife's conscience.

These are the statutes which the LORD commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between the father and his daughter, being yet in her youth in her father's house. Guidance: A short summary, but worth pausing on. God writes household law into His Torah — He is not above the domestic. The faith does not treat the kitchen and the bedroom as lower territory than the altar. Whose word holds and whose word releases — He cares.
